name: The Grid AI

dataResidency.euRegionDetails: The Grid states that API requests are routed to the lowest-cost qualifying provider/model, and its docs contemplate multiple suppliers plus possible future "regional privacy constraints," but no current primary-source documentation was found that guarantees EU-only processing or identifies EU processing regions.

dataUsage.retentionPolicy: The app states that all instruments have zero data retention. Separately, The Grid documents that it records usage/metering data for every inference call, including timestamp, token counts, unit/lot information, trade details, supplier information, and performance metrics; no retention duration for those records was found.
dataUsage.details: The Grid routes /chat/completions requests to the lowest-cost provider/model satisfying the instrument requirements, and records routing/metering data for auditing and billing.

notes: Primary-source public compliance documentation appears very limited: no privacy policy, DPA/AVV, SCC statement, or public sub-processor list was found on the provider’s site or docs as of April 11, 2026. The strongest documented privacy-related claims are zero data retention for instruments plus detailed metering/routing records, and the product architecture indicates inference may be routed across competing suppliers rather than being confined to the EU.
